mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates compress equity valuation multiples by increasing the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, liquidity constraints tighten across financial markets, triggering rapid deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and deceleration in payroll employment signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces no statistically significant directional bias (p > 0.10) in 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, accelerating real GDP growth outpaces capacity constraints, prompting forward-looking equity investors to price in imminent monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle expansion periods when GDPC1 growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth expands corporate revenue bases and improves earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates a shift in consumer demand toward services and away from durable goods, creating sector rotation that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-HOUST divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0 percent tightens labor markets, forcing firms to raise wages and compress margins, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0 percent during recovery phases.
